1→// Copyright (C) NHR@FAU, University Erlangen-Nuremberg.
     2→// All rights reserved. This file is part of cc-backend.
     3→// Use of this source code is governed by a MIT-style
     4→// license that can be found in the LICENSE file.
     5→
     6→// Package metricstore provides walCheckpoint.go: WAL-based checkpoint implementation.
     7→//
     8→// This replaces the Avro shadow tree with an append-only Write-Ahead Log (WAL)
     9→// per host, eliminating the extra memory overhead of the AvroStore and providing
    10→// truly continuous (per-write) crash safety.
    11→//
    12→// # Architecture
    13→//
    14→//	Metric write (DecodeLine)
    15→//	      │
    16→//	      ├─► WriteToLevel()    → main MemoryStore (unchanged)
    17→//	      │
    18→//	      └─► WALMessages channel
    19→//	               │
    20→//	               ▼
    21→//	        WALStaging goroutine
    22→//	               │
    23→//	               ▼
    24→//	        checkpoints/cluster/host/current.wal  (append-only, binary)
    25→//
    26→//	Periodic checkpoint (Checkpointing goroutine):
    27→//	  1. Write <timestamp>.bin snapshot (column-oriented, from main tree)
    28→//	  2. Signal WALStaging to truncate current.wal per host
    29→//
    30→//	On restart (FromCheckpoint):
    31→//	  1. Load most recent <timestamp>.bin snapshot
    32→//	  2. Replay current.wal (overwrite-safe: buffer.write handles duplicate timestamps)
    33→//
    34→// # WAL Record Format
    35→//
    36→//	[4B magic 0xCC1DA7A1][4B payload_len][payload][4B CRC32]
    37→//
    38→//	payload:
    39→//	  [8B timestamp int64]
    40→//	  [2B metric_name_len uint16][N metric name bytes]
    41→//	  [1B selector_count uint8]
    42→//	  per selector: [1B selector_len uint8][M selector bytes]
    43→//	  [4B value float32 bits]
    44→//
    45→// # Binary Snapshot Format
    46→//
    47→//	[4B magic 0xCC5B0001][8B from int64][8B to int64]
    48→//	Level tree (recursive):
    49→//	  [4B num_metrics uint32]
    50→//	  per metric:
    51→//	    [2B name_len uint16][N name bytes]
    52→//	    [8B frequency int64][8B start int64]
    53→//	    [4B num_values uint32][num_values × 4B float32]
    54→//	  [4B num_children uint32]
    55→//	  per child: [2B name_len uint16][N name bytes] + Level (recursive)
    56→package metricstore
    57→
    58→import (
    59→	"bufio"
    60→	"context"
    61→	"encoding/binary"
    62→	"fmt"
    63→	"hash/crc32"
    64→	"hash/fnv"
    65→	"io"
    66→	"math"
    67→	"os"
    68→	"path"
    69→	"strings"
    70→	"sync"
    71→	"sync/atomic"
    72→	"time"
    73→
    74→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    75→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    76→)
    77→
    78→// Magic numbers for binary formats.
    79→const (
    80→	walFileMagic   = uint32(0xCC1DA701) // WAL file header magic
    81→	walRecordMagic = uint32(0xCC1DA7A1) // WAL record magic
    82→	snapFileMagic  = uint32(0xCC5B0001) // Binary snapshot magic
    83→)
    84→
    85→// walShardChs holds per-shard channels for WAL messages.
    86→// Initialized by WALStaging; nil when WAL is not active.
    87→var walShardChs []chan *WALMessage
    88→
    89→// walShardRotateChs holds per-shard channels for WAL rotation requests.
    90→var walShardRotateChs []chan walRotateReq
    91→
    92→// walNumShards stores the number of shards (set during WALStaging init).
    93→var walNumShards int
    94→
    95→// walStagingWg tracks WALStaging goroutine exits for shutdown synchronization.
    96→var walStagingWg sync.WaitGroup
    97→
    98→// WALMessage represents a single metric write to be appended to the WAL.
    99→// Cluster and Node are NOT stored in the WAL record (inferred from file path).
   100→type WALMessage struct {
   101→	MetricName string
   102→	Cluster    string
   103→	Node       string
   104→	Selector   []string
   105→	Value      schema.Float
   106→	Timestamp  int64
   107→}
   108→
   109→// walRotateReq requests WAL file rotation for a specific host directory.
   110→// The done channel is closed by the WAL goroutine when rotation is complete.
   111→type walRotateReq struct {
   112→	hostDir string
   113→	done    chan struct{}
   114→}
   115→
   116→// walFileState holds an open WAL file handle and buffered writer for one host directory.
   117→type walFileState struct {
   118→	f     *os.File
   119→	w     *bufio.Writer
   120→	dirty bool
   121→}
   122→
   123→// walFlushInterval controls how often dirty WAL files are flushed to disk.
   124→// Decoupling flushes from message processing lets the consumer run at memory
   125→// speed, amortizing syscall overhead across many writes.
   126→const walFlushInterval = 5 * time.Second
   127→
   128→// walShardIndex computes which shard a message belongs to based on cluster+node.
   129→// Uses FNV-1a hash for fast, well-distributed mapping.
   130→func walShardIndex(cluster, node string) int {
   131→	h := fnv.New32a()
   132→	h.Write([]byte(cluster))
   133→	h.Write([]byte{0})
   134→	h.Write([]byte(node))
   135→	return int(h.Sum32()) % walNumShards
   136→}
   137→
   138→// SendWALMessage routes a WAL message to the appropriate shard channel.
   139→// Returns false if the channel is full (message dropped).
   140→func SendWALMessage(msg *WALMessage) bool {
   141→	if walShardChs == nil {
   142→		return false
   143→	}
   144→	shard := walShardIndex(msg.Cluster, msg.Node)
   145→	select {
   146→	case walShardChs[shard] <- msg:
   147→		return true
   148→	default:
   149→		return false
   150→	}
   151→}
   152→
   153→// WALStaging starts N sharded background goroutines that receive WALMessage items
   154→// and append binary WAL records to per-host current.wal files.
   155→// Each shard owns a subset of hosts (determined by hash of cluster+node),
   156→// parallelizing serialization and I/O while keeping per-host writes serial.
   157→func WALStaging(wg *sync.WaitGroup, ctx context.Context) {
   158→	if Keys.Checkpoints.FileFormat == "json" {
   159→		return
   160→	}
   161→
   162→	walNumShards = max(Keys.NumWorkers, 1)
   163→	chBufSize := max(65536/walNumShards, 1024)
   164→
   165→	walShardChs = make([]chan *WALMessage, walNumShards)
   166→	walShardRotateChs = make([]chan walRotateReq, walNumShards)
   167→
   168→	for i := range walNumShards {
   169→		walShardChs[i] = make(chan *WALMessage, chBufSize)
   170→		walShardRotateChs[i] = make(chan walRotateReq, 64)
   171→	}
   172→
   173→	for i := range walNumShards {
   174→		msgCh := walShardChs[i]
   175→		rotateCh := walShardRotateChs[i]
   176→
   177→		walStagingWg.Add(1)
   178→		wg.Go(func() {
   179→			defer walStagingWg.Done()
   180→			hostFiles := make(map[string]*walFileState)
   181→
   182→			defer func() {
   183→				for _, ws := range hostFiles {
   184→					if ws.f != nil {
   185→						ws.w.Flush()
   186→						ws.f.Close()
   187→					}
   188→				}
   189→			}()
   190→
   191→			getOrOpenWAL := func(hostDir string) *walFileState {
   192→				ws, ok := hostFiles[hostDir]
   193→				if ok {
   194→					return ws
   195→				}
   196→
   197→				if err := os.MkdirAll(hostDir, CheckpointDirPerms); err != nil {
   198→					cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: mkdir %s: %v", hostDir, err)
   199→					return nil
   200→				}
   201→
   202→				walPath := path.Join(hostDir, "current.wal")
   203→				f, err := os.OpenFile(walPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, CheckpointFilePerms)
   204→				if err != nil {
   205→					cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: open %s: %v", walPath, err)
   206→					return nil
   207→				}
   208→
   209→				w := bufio.NewWriter(f)
   210→
   211→				// Write file header magic if file is new (empty).
   212→				info, err := f.Stat()
   213→				if err == nil && info.Size() == 0 {
   214→					var hdr [4]byte
   215→					binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(hdr[:], walFileMagic)
   216→					if _, err := w.Write(hdr[:]); err != nil {
   217→						cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: write header %s: %v", walPath, err)
   218→						f.Close()
   219→						return nil
   220→					}
   221→				}
   222→
   223→				ws = &walFileState{f: f, w: w}
   224→				hostFiles[hostDir] = ws
   225→				return ws
   226→			}
   227→
   228→			processMsg := func(msg *WALMessage) {
   229→				hostDir := path.Join(Keys.Checkpoints.RootDir, msg.Cluster, msg.Node)
   230→				ws := getOrOpenWAL(hostDir)
   231→				if ws == nil {
   232→					return
   233→				}
   234→				if err := writeWALRecordDirect(ws.w, msg); err != nil {
   235→					cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: write record: %v", err)
   236→				}
   237→				ws.dirty = true
   238→			}
   239→
   240→			processRotate := func(req walRotateReq) {
   241→				ws, ok := hostFiles[req.hostDir]
   242→				if ok && ws.f != nil {
   243→					ws.w.Flush()
   244→					ws.f.Close()
   245→					walPath := path.Join(req.hostDir, "current.wal")
   246→					if err := os.Remove(walPath); err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   247→						cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: remove %s: %v", walPath, err)
   248→					}
   249→					delete(hostFiles, req.hostDir)
   250→				}
   251→				close(req.done)
   252→			}
   253→
   254→			flushDirty := func() {
   255→				for _, ws := range hostFiles {
   256→					if ws.dirty {
   257→						ws.w.Flush()
   258→						ws.dirty = false
   259→					}
   260→				}
   261→			}
   262→
   263→			ticker := time.NewTicker(walFlushInterval)
   264→			defer ticker.Stop()
   265→
   266→			// drainBatch processes up to 4096 pending messages without blocking.
   267→			// Returns false if the channel was closed.
   268→			drainBatch := func() bool {
   269→				for range 4096 {
   270→					select {
   271→					case msg, ok := <-msgCh:
   272→						if !ok {
   273→							flushDirty()
   274→							return false
   275→						}
   276→						processMsg(msg)
   277→					case req := <-rotateCh:
   278→						processRotate(req)
   279→					default:
   280→						return true
   281→					}
   282→				}
   283→				return true
   284→			}
   285→
   286→			for {
   287→				select {
   288→				case <-ctx.Done():
   289→					// On shutdown, skip draining buffered messages — a full binary
   290→					// checkpoint will be written from in-memory state, making
   291→					// buffered WAL records redundant.
   292→					flushDirty()
   293→					return
   294→				case msg, ok := <-msgCh:
   295→					if !ok {
   296→						return
   297→					}
   298→					processMsg(msg)
   299→					if !drainBatch() {
   300→						return
   301→					}
   302→					// No flush here — timer handles periodic flushing.
   303→				case <-ticker.C:
   304→					flushDirty()
   305→				case req := <-rotateCh:
   306→					processRotate(req)
   307→				}
   308→			}
   309→		})
   310→	}
   311→}
   312→
   313→// WaitForWALStagingDrain blocks until all WALStaging goroutines have exited.
   314→// Must be called after closing walShardChs to ensure all file handles are
   315→// flushed and closed before checkpoint writes begin.
   316→func WaitForWALStagingDrain() {
   317→	walStagingWg.Wait()
   318→}
   319→
   320→// RotateWALFiles sends rotation requests for the given host directories
   321→// and blocks until all rotations complete. Each request is routed to the
   322→// shard that owns the host directory.
   323→func RotateWALFiles(hostDirs []string) {
   324→	if walShardRotateChs == nil {
   325→		return
   326→	}
   327→	dones := make([]chan struct{}, len(hostDirs))
   328→	for i, dir := range hostDirs {
   329→		dones[i] = make(chan struct{})
   330→		// Extract cluster/node from hostDir to find the right shard.
   331→		// hostDir = rootDir/cluster/node
   332→		shard := walShardIndexFromDir(dir)
   333→		walShardRotateChs[shard] <- walRotateReq{hostDir: dir, done: dones[i]}
   334→	}
   335→	for _, done := range dones {
   336→		<-done
   337→	}
   338→}
   339→
   340→// walShardIndexFromDir extracts cluster and node from a host directory path
   341→// and returns the shard index. The path format is: rootDir/cluster/node
   342→func walShardIndexFromDir(hostDir string) int {
   343→	// hostDir = .../cluster/node — extract last two path components
   344→	node := path.Base(hostDir)
   345→	cluster := path.Base(path.Dir(hostDir))
   346→	return walShardIndex(cluster, node)
   347→}
   348→
   349→// RotateWALFiles sends rotation requests for the given host directories
   350→// and blocks until all rotations complete.
   351→func RotateWALFilesAfterShutdown(hostDirs []string) {
   352→	for _, dir := range hostDirs {
   353→		walPath := path.Join(dir, "current.wal")
   354→		if err := os.Remove(walPath); err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   355→			cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: remove %s: %v", walPath, err)
   356→		}
   357→	}
   358→}
   359→
   360→// writeWALRecordDirect encodes a WAL record directly into the bufio.Writer,
   361→// avoiding heap allocations by using a stack-allocated scratch buffer for
   362→// the fixed-size header/trailer and computing CRC inline.
   363→func writeWALRecordDirect(w *bufio.Writer, msg *WALMessage) error {
   364→	// Compute payload size.
   365→	payloadSize := 8 + 2 + len(msg.MetricName) + 1 + 4
   366→	for _, s := range msg.Selector {
   367→		payloadSize += 1 + len(s)
   368→	}
   369→
   370→	// Write magic + payload length (8 bytes header).
   371→	var hdr [8]byte
   372→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(hdr[0:4], walRecordMagic)
   373→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(hdr[4:8], uint32(payloadSize))
   374→	if _, err := w.Write(hdr[:]); err != nil {
   375→		return err
   376→	}
   377→
   378→	// We need to compute CRC over the payload as we write it.
   379→	crc := crc32.NewIEEE()
   380→
   381→	// Timestamp (8 bytes).
   382→	var scratch [8]byte
   383→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(scratch[:8], uint64(msg.Timestamp))
   384→	crc.Write(scratch[:8])
   385→	if _, err := w.Write(scratch[:8]); err != nil {
   386→		return err
   387→	}
   388→
   389→	// Metric name length (2 bytes) + metric name.
   390→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint16(scratch[:2], uint16(len(msg.MetricName)))
   391→	crc.Write(scratch[:2])
   392→	if _, err := w.Write(scratch[:2]); err != nil {
   393→		return err
   394→	}
   395→	nameBytes := []byte(msg.MetricName)
   396→	crc.Write(nameBytes)
   397→	if _, err := w.Write(nameBytes); err != nil {
   398→		return err
   399→	}
   400→
   401→	// Selector count (1 byte).
   402→	scratch[0] = byte(len(msg.Selector))
   403→	crc.Write(scratch[:1])
   404→	if _, err := w.Write(scratch[:1]); err != nil {
   405→		return err
   406→	}
   407→
   408→	// Selectors (1-byte length + bytes each).
   409→	for _, sel := range msg.Selector {
   410→		scratch[0] = byte(len(sel))
   411→		crc.Write(scratch[:1])
   412→		if _, err := w.Write(scratch[:1]); err != nil {
   413→			return err
   414→		}
   415→		selBytes := []byte(sel)
   416→		crc.Write(selBytes)
   417→		if _, err := w.Write(selBytes); err != nil {
   418→			return err
   419→		}
   420→	}
   421→
   422→	// Value (4 bytes, float32 bits).
   423→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(scratch[:4], math.Float32bits(float32(msg.Value)))
   424→	crc.Write(scratch[:4])
   425→	if _, err := w.Write(scratch[:4]); err != nil {
   426→		return err
   427→	}
   428→
   429→	// CRC32 (4 bytes).
   430→	binary.LittleEndian.PutUint32(scratch[:4], crc.Sum32())
   431→	_, err := w.Write(scratch[:4])
   432→	return err
   433→}
   434→
   435→// readWALRecord reads one WAL record from the reader.
   436→// Returns (nil, nil) on clean EOF. Returns error on data corruption.
   437→// A CRC mismatch indicates a truncated trailing record (expected on crash).
   438→func readWALRecord(r io.Reader) (*WALMessage, error) {
   439→	var magic uint32
   440→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &magic); err != nil {
   441→		if err == io.EOF {
   442→			return nil, nil // Clean EOF
   443→		}
   444→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read record magic: %w", err)
   445→	}
   446→
   447→	if magic != walRecordMagic {
   448→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid record magic 0x%08X (expected 0x%08X)", magic, walRecordMagic)
   449→	}
   450→
   451→	var payloadLen uint32
   452→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &payloadLen); err != nil {
   453→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read payload length: %w", err)
   454→	}
   455→
   456→	if payloadLen > 1<<20 { // 1 MB sanity limit
   457→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("record payload too large: %d bytes", payloadLen)
   458→	}
   459→
   460→	payload := make([]byte, payloadLen)
   461→	if _, err := io.ReadFull(r, payload); err != nil {
   462→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read payload: %w", err)
   463→	}
   464→
   465→	var storedCRC uint32
   466→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &storedCRC); err != nil {
   467→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read CRC: %w", err)
   468→	}
   469→
   470→	if crc32.ChecksumIEEE(payload) != storedCRC {
   471→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("CRC mismatch (truncated write or corruption)")
   472→	}
   473→
   474→	return parseWALPayload(payload)
   475→}
   476→
   477→// parseWALPayload decodes a binary payload into a WALMessage.
   478→func parseWALPayload(payload []byte) (*WALMessage, error) {
   479→	if len(payload) < 8+2+1+4 {
   480→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("payload too short: %d bytes", len(payload))
   481→	}
   482→
   483→	offset := 0
   484→
   485→	// Timestamp (8 bytes)
   486→	ts := int64(binary.LittleEndian.Uint64(payload[offset : offset+8]))
   487→	offset += 8
   488→
   489→	// Metric name (2-byte length + bytes)
   490→	if offset+2 > len(payload) {
   491→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("metric name length overflows payload")
   492→	}
   493→	mLen := int(binary.LittleEndian.Uint16(payload[offset : offset+2]))
   494→	offset += 2
   495→
   496→	if offset+mLen > len(payload) {
   497→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("metric name overflows payload")
   498→	}
   499→	metricName := string(payload[offset : offset+mLen])
   500→	offset += mLen
   501→
   502→	// Selector count (1 byte)
   503→	if offset >= len(payload) {
   504→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("selector count overflows payload")
   505→	}
   506→	selCount := int(payload[offset])
   507→	offset++
   508→
   509→	selectors := make([]string, selCount)
   510→	for i := range selCount {
   511→		if offset >= len(payload) {
   512→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("selector[%d] length overflows payload", i)
   513→		}
   514→		sLen := int(payload[offset])
   515→		offset++
   516→
   517→		if offset+sLen > len(payload) {
   518→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("selector[%d] data overflows payload", i)
   519→		}
   520→		selectors[i] = string(payload[offset : offset+sLen])
   521→		offset += sLen
   522→	}
   523→
   524→	// Value (4 bytes, float32 bits)
   525→	if offset+4 > len(payload) {
   526→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("value overflows payload")
   527→	}
   528→	bits := binary.LittleEndian.Uint32(payload[offset : offset+4])
   529→	value := schema.Float(math.Float32frombits(bits))
   530→
   531→	return &WALMessage{
   532→		MetricName: metricName,
   533→		Timestamp:  ts,
   534→		Selector:   selectors,
   535→		Value:      value,
   536→	}, nil
   537→}
   538→
   539→// loadWALFile reads a WAL file and replays all valid records into the Level tree.
   540→// l is the host-level node. Corrupt or partial trailing records are silently skipped
   541→// (expected on crash). Records older than 'from' are skipped.
   542→func (l *Level) loadWALFile(m *MemoryStore, f *os.File, from int64) error {
   543→	br := bufio.NewReader(f)
   544→
   545→	// Verify file header magic.
   546→	var fileMagic uint32
   547→	if err := binary.Read(br, binary.LittleEndian, &fileMagic); err != nil {
   548→		if err == io.EOF {
   549→			return nil // Empty file, no data
   550→		}
   551→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: read file header: %w", err)
   552→	}
   553→
   554→	if fileMagic != walFileMagic {
   555→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: invalid file magic 0x%08X (expected 0x%08X)", fileMagic, walFileMagic)
   556→	}
   557→
   558→	// Cache level lookups to avoid repeated tree traversal.
   559→	lvlCache := make(map[string]*Level)
   560→
   561→	for {
   562→		msg, err := readWALRecord(br)
   563→		if err != nil {
   564→			// Truncated trailing record is expected after a crash; stop replaying.
   565→			cclog.Debugf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL: stopping replay at corrupted/partial record: %v", err)
   566→			break
   567→		}
   568→		if msg == nil {
   569→			break // Clean EOF
   570→		}
   571→
   572→		if msg.Timestamp < from {
   573→			continue // Older than retention window
   574→		}
   575→
   576→		minfo, ok := m.Metrics[msg.MetricName]
   577→		if !ok {
   578→			continue // Unknown metric (config may have changed)
   579→		}
   580→
   581→		// Cache key is the null-separated selector path.
   582→		cacheKey := joinSelector(msg.Selector)
   583→		lvl, ok := lvlCache[cacheKey]
   584→		if !ok {
   585→			lvl = l.findLevelOrCreate(msg.Selector, len(m.Metrics))
   586→			lvlCache[cacheKey] = lvl
   587→		}
   588→
   589→		// Write directly to the buffer, same as WriteToLevel but without the
   590→		// global level lookup (we already have the right level).
   591→		lvl.lock.Lock()
   592→		b := lvl.metrics[minfo.offset]
   593→		if b == nil {
   594→			b = newBuffer(msg.Timestamp, minfo.Frequency)
   595→			lvl.metrics[minfo.offset] = b
   596→		}
   597→		nb, writeErr := b.write(msg.Timestamp, msg.Value)
   598→		if writeErr == nil && b != nb {
   599→			lvl.metrics[minfo.offset] = nb
   600→		}
   601→		// Ignore write errors for timestamps before buffer start (can happen when
   602→		// replaying WAL entries that predate a loaded snapshot's start time).
   603→		lvl.lock.Unlock()
   604→	}
   605→
   606→	return nil
   607→}
   608→
   609→// joinSelector builds a cache key from a selector slice using null bytes as separators.
   610→func joinSelector(sel []string) string {
   611→	if len(sel) == 0 {
   612→		return ""
   613→	}
   614→	var result strings.Builder
   615→	result.WriteString(sel[0])
   616→	for i := 1; i < len(sel); i++ {
   617→		result.WriteString("\x00" + sel[i])
   618→	}
   619→	return result.String()
   620→}
   621→
   622→// ToCheckpointWAL writes binary snapshot files for all hosts in parallel.
   623→// Returns the number of files written, the list of host directories that were
   624→// successfully checkpointed (for WAL rotation), and any errors.
   625→func (m *MemoryStore) ToCheckpointWAL(dir string, from, to int64) (int, []string, error) {
   626→	// Collect all cluster/host pairs.
   627→	m.root.lock.RLock()
   628→	totalHosts := 0
   629→	for _, l1 := range m.root.children {
   630→		l1.lock.RLock()
   631→		totalHosts += len(l1.children)
   632→		l1.lock.RUnlock()
   633→	}
   634→	m.root.lock.RUnlock()
   635→
   636→	levels := make([]*Level, 0, totalHosts)
   637→	selectors := make([][]string, 0, totalHosts)
   638→
   639→	m.root.lock.RLock()
   640→	for sel1, l1 := range m.root.children {
   641→		l1.lock.RLock()
   642→		for sel2, l2 := range l1.children {
   643→			levels = append(levels, l2)
   644→			selectors = append(selectors, []string{sel1, sel2})
   645→		}
   646→		l1.lock.RUnlock()
   647→	}
   648→	m.root.lock.RUnlock()
   649→
   650→	type workItem struct {
   651→		level    *Level
   652→		hostDir  string
   653→		selector []string
   654→	}
   655→
   656→	totalWork := len(levels)
   657→	cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> Starting binary checkpoint for %d hosts with %d workers", totalWork, Keys.NumWorkers)
   658→
   659→	n, errs, completed := int32(0), int32(0), int32(0)
   660→	var successDirs []string
   661→	var successMu sync.Mutex
   662→
   663→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
   664→	wg.Add(Keys.NumWorkers)
   665→	work := make(chan workItem, Keys.NumWorkers*2)
   666→
   667→	// Progress logging goroutine.
   668→	stopProgress := make(chan struct{})
   669→	go func() {
   670→		ticker := time.NewTicker(10 * time.Second)
   671→		defer ticker.Stop()
   672→		for {
   673→			select {
   674→			case <-ticker.C:
   675→				cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> Checkpoint progress: %d/%d hosts (%d written, %d errors)",
   676→					atomic.LoadInt32(&completed), totalWork, atomic.LoadInt32(&n), atomic.LoadInt32(&errs))
   677→			case <-stopProgress:
   678→				return
   679→			}
   680→		}
   681→	}()
   682→
   683→	for range Keys.NumWorkers {
   684→		go func() {
   685→			defer wg.Done()
   686→			for wi := range work {
   687→				err := wi.level.toCheckpointBinary(wi.hostDir, from, to, m)
   688→				if err != nil {
   689→					if err == ErrNoNewArchiveData {
   690→						atomic.AddInt32(&completed, 1)
   691→						continue
   692→					}
   693→					cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary checkpoint error for %s: %v", wi.hostDir, err)
   694→					atomic.AddInt32(&errs, 1)
   695→				} else {
   696→					atomic.AddInt32(&n, 1)
   697→					// Delete WAL immediately after successful snapshot.
   698→					walPath := path.Join(wi.hostDir, "current.wal")
   699→					if err := os.Remove(walPath); err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   700→						cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL remove %s: %v", walPath, err)
   701→					}
   702→					successMu.Lock()
   703→					successDirs = append(successDirs, wi.hostDir)
   704→					successMu.Unlock()
   705→				}
   706→				atomic.AddInt32(&completed, 1)
   707→			}
   708→		}()
   709→	}
   710→
   711→	for i := range levels {
   712→		hostDir := path.Join(dir, path.Join(selectors[i]...))
   713→		work <- workItem{
   714→			level:    levels[i],
   715→			hostDir:  hostDir,
   716→			selector: selectors[i],
   717→		}
   718→	}
   719→	close(work)
   720→	wg.Wait()
   721→	close(stopProgress)
   722→
   723→	if errs > 0 {
   724→		return int(n), successDirs, fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> %d errors during binary checkpoint (%d successes)", errs, n)
   725→	}
   726→	return int(n), successDirs, nil
   727→}
   728→
   729→// toCheckpointBinary writes a binary snapshot file for a single host-level node.
   730→// Uses atomic rename (write to .tmp then rename) to avoid partial reads on crash.
   731→func (l *Level) toCheckpointBinary(dir string, from, to int64, m *MemoryStore) error {
   732→	cf, err := l.toCheckpointFile(from, to, m)
   733→	if err != nil {
   734→		return err
   735→	}
   736→	if cf == nil {
   737→		return ErrNoNewArchiveData
   738→	}
   739→
   740→	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, CheckpointDirPerms); err != nil {
   741→		return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", dir, err)
   742→	}
   743→
   744→	// Write to a temp file first, then rename (atomic on POSIX).
   745→	tmpPath := path.Join(dir, fmt.Sprintf("%d.bin.tmp", from))
   746→	finalPath := path.Join(dir, fmt.Sprintf("%d.bin", from))
   747→
   748→	f, err := os.OpenFile(tmpPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, CheckpointFilePerms)
   749→	if err != nil {
   750→		return fmt.Errorf("open binary snapshot %s: %w", tmpPath, err)
   751→	}
   752→
   753→	bw := bufio.NewWriter(f)
   754→	if err := writeBinarySnapshotFile(bw, cf); err != nil {
   755→		f.Close()
   756→		os.Remove(tmpPath)
   757→		return fmt.Errorf("write binary snapshot: %w", err)
   758→	}
   759→	if err := bw.Flush(); err != nil {
   760→		f.Close()
   761→		os.Remove(tmpPath)
   762→		return err
   763→	}
   764→	f.Close()
   765→
   766→	return os.Rename(tmpPath, finalPath)
   767→}
   768→
   769→// writeBinarySnapshotFile writes the binary snapshot file header and level tree.
   770→func writeBinarySnapshotFile(w io.Writer, cf *CheckpointFile) error {
   771→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, snapFileMagic); err != nil {
   772→		return err
   773→	}
   774→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, cf.From); err != nil {
   775→		return err
   776→	}
   777→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, cf.To); err != nil {
   778→		return err
   779→	}
   780→	return writeBinaryLevel(w, cf)
   781→}
   782→
   783→// writeBinaryLevel recursively writes a CheckpointFile level in binary format.
   784→func writeBinaryLevel(w io.Writer, cf *CheckpointFile) error {
   785→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, uint32(len(cf.Metrics))); err != nil {
   786→		return err
   787→	}
   788→
   789→	for name, metric := range cf.Metrics {
   790→		if err := writeString16(w, name); err != nil {
   791→			return err
   792→		}
   793→		if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, metric.Frequency); err != nil {
   794→			return err
   795→		}
   796→		if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, metric.Start); err != nil {
   797→			return err
   798→		}
   799→		if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, uint32(len(metric.Data))); err != nil {
   800→			return err
   801→		}
   802→		for _, v := range metric.Data {
   803→			if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, math.Float32bits(float32(v))); err != nil {
   804→				return err
   805→			}
   806→		}
   807→	}
   808→
   809→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, uint32(len(cf.Children))); err != nil {
   810→		return err
   811→	}
   812→
   813→	for name, child := range cf.Children {
   814→		if err := writeString16(w, name); err != nil {
   815→			return err
   816→		}
   817→		if err := writeBinaryLevel(w, child); err != nil {
   818→			return err
   819→		}
   820→	}
   821→
   822→	return nil
   823→}
   824→
   825→// writeString16 writes a 2-byte length-prefixed string to w.
   826→func writeString16(w io.Writer, s string) error {
   827→	if err := binary.Write(w, binary.LittleEndian, uint16(len(s))); err != nil {
   828→		return err
   829→	}
   830→	_, err := io.WriteString(w, s)
   831→	return err
   832→}
   833→
   834→// loadBinaryFile reads a binary snapshot file and loads data into the Level tree.
   835→// The retention check (from) is applied to the file's 'to' timestamp.
   836→func (l *Level) loadBinaryFile(m *MemoryStore, f *os.File, from int64) error {
   837→	br := bufio.NewReader(f)
   838→
   839→	var magic uint32
   840→	if err := binary.Read(br, binary.LittleEndian, &magic); err != nil {
   841→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: read magic: %w", err)
   842→	}
   843→	if magic != snapFileMagic {
   844→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: invalid magic 0x%08X (expected 0x%08X)", magic, snapFileMagic)
   845→	}
   846→
   847→	var fileFrom, fileTo int64
   848→	if err := binary.Read(br, binary.LittleEndian, &fileFrom); err != nil {
   849→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: read from: %w", err)
   850→	}
   851→	if err := binary.Read(br, binary.LittleEndian, &fileTo); err != nil {
   852→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: read to: %w", err)
   853→	}
   854→
   855→	if fileTo != 0 && fileTo < from {
   856→		return nil // File is older than retention window, skip it
   857→	}
   858→
   859→	cf, err := readBinaryLevel(br)
   860→	if err != nil {
   861→		return fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> binary snapshot: read level tree: %w", err)
   862→	}
   863→	cf.From = fileFrom
   864→	cf.To = fileTo
   865→
   866→	return l.loadFile(cf, m)
   867→}
   868→
   869→// readBinaryLevel recursively reads a level from the binary snapshot format.
   870→func readBinaryLevel(r io.Reader) (*CheckpointFile, error) {
   871→	cf := &CheckpointFile{
   872→		Metrics:  make(map[string]*CheckpointMetrics),
   873→		Children: make(map[string]*CheckpointFile),
   874→	}
   875→
   876→	var numMetrics uint32
   877→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &numMetrics); err != nil {
   878→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read num_metrics: %w", err)
   879→	}
   880→
   881→	for range numMetrics {
   882→		name, err := readString16(r)
   883→		if err != nil {
   884→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read metric name: %w", err)
   885→		}
   886→
   887→		var freq, start int64
   888→		if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &freq); err != nil {
   889→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read frequency for %s: %w", name, err)
   890→		}
   891→		if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &start); err != nil {
   892→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read start for %s: %w", name, err)
   893→		}
   894→
   895→		var numValues uint32
   896→		if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &numValues); err != nil {
   897→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read num_values for %s: %w", name, err)
   898→		}
   899→
   900→		data := make([]schema.Float, numValues)
   901→		for i := range numValues {
   902→			var bits uint32
   903→			if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &bits); err != nil {
   904→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("read value[%d] for %s: %w", i, name, err)
   905→			}
   906→			data[i] = schema.Float(math.Float32frombits(bits))
   907→		}
   908→
   909→		cf.Metrics[name] = &CheckpointMetrics{
   910→			Frequency: freq,
   911→			Start:     start,
   912→			Data:      data,
   913→		}
   914→	}
   915→
   916→	var numChildren uint32
   917→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &numChildren); err != nil {
   918→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read num_children: %w", err)
   919→	}
   920→
   921→	for range numChildren {
   922→		childName, err := readString16(r)
   923→		if err != nil {
   924→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read child name: %w", err)
   925→		}
   926→
   927→		child, err := readBinaryLevel(r)
   928→		if err != nil {
   929→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read child %s: %w", childName, err)
   930→		}
   931→		cf.Children[childName] = child
   932→	}
   933→
   934→	return cf, nil
   935→}
   936→
   937→// readString16 reads a 2-byte length-prefixed string from r.
   938→func readString16(r io.Reader) (string, error) {
   939→	var sLen uint16
   940→	if err := binary.Read(r, binary.LittleEndian, &sLen); err != nil {
   941→		return "", err
   942→	}
   943→	buf := make([]byte, sLen)
   944→	if _, err := io.ReadFull(r, buf); err != nil {
   945→		return "", err
   946→	}
   947→	return string(buf), nil
   948→}

     5→
     6→// This file implements ingestion of InfluxDB line-protocol metric data received
     7→// over NATS. Each line encodes one metric sample with the following structure:
     8→//
     9→//	<measurement>[,cluster=<c>][,hostname=<h>][,type=<t>][,type-id=<id>][,subtype=<s>][,stype-id=<id>] value=<v> [<timestamp>]
    10→//
    11→// The measurement name identifies the metric (e.g. "cpu_load"). Tags provide
    12→// routing information (cluster, host) and optional sub-device selectors (type,
    13→// subtype). Only one field is expected per line: "value".
    14→//
    15→// After decoding, each sample is:
    16→//  1. Written to the in-memory store via ms.WriteToLevel.
    17→//  2. If the checkpoint format is "wal", also forwarded to the WAL staging
    18→//     goroutine via the WALMessages channel for durable write-ahead logging.
    19→package metricstore
    20→
    21→import (
    22→	"bytes"
    23→	"context"
    24→	"fmt"
    25→	"sync"
    26→	"sync/atomic"
    27→	"time"
    28→
    29→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    30→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/nats"
    31→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    32→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-line-protocol/v2/lineprotocol"
    33→)
    34→
    35→// ReceiveNats subscribes to all configured NATS subjects and feeds incoming
    36→// line-protocol messages into the MemoryStore.
    37→//
    38→// When workers > 1 a pool of goroutines drains a shared channel so that
    39→// multiple messages can be decoded in parallel. With workers == 1 the NATS
    40→// callback decodes inline (no channel overhead, lower latency).
    41→//
    42→// The function blocks until ctx is cancelled and all worker goroutines have
    43→// finished. It returns nil when the NATS client is not configured; callers
    44→// should treat that as a no-op rather than an error.
    45→func ReceiveNats(ms *MemoryStore,
    46→	workers int,
    47→	ctx context.Context,
    48→) error {
    49→	nc := nats.GetClient()
    50→
    51→	if nc == nil {
    52→		cclog.Warn("NATS client not initialized")
    53→		return nil
    54→	}
    55→
    56→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
    57→	msgs := make(chan []byte, max(workers*256, 8192))
    58→
    59→	for _, sc := range *Keys.Subscriptions {
    60→		clusterTag := sc.ClusterTag
    61→		if workers > 1 {
    62→			wg.Add(workers)
    63→
    64→			for range workers {
    65→				go func() {
    66→					defer wg.Done()
    67→					for m := range msgs {
    68→						dec := lineprotocol.NewDecoderWithBytes(m)
    69→						if err := DecodeLine(dec, ms, clusterTag); err != nil {
    70→							cclog.Errorf("error: %s", err.Error())
    71→						}
    72→					}
    73→				}()
    74→			}
    75→
    76→			nc.Subscribe(sc.SubscribeTo, func(subject string, data []byte) {
    77→				select {
    78→				case msgs <- data:
    79→				case <-ctx.Done():
    80→				}
    81→			})
    82→		} else {
    83→			nc.Subscribe(sc.SubscribeTo, func(subject string, data []byte) {
    84→				dec := lineprotocol.NewDecoderWithBytes(data)
    85→				if err := DecodeLine(dec, ms, clusterTag); err != nil {
    86→					cclog.Errorf("error: %s", err.Error())
    87→				}
    88→			})
    89→		}
    90→		cclog.Infof("NATS subscription to '%s' established", sc.SubscribeTo)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	go func() {
    94→		<-ctx.Done()
    95→		close(msgs)
    96→	}()
    97→
    98→	wg.Wait()
    99→
   100→	return nil
   101→}
   102→
   103→// reorder prepends prefix to buf in-place when buf has enough spare capacity,
   104→// avoiding an allocation. Falls back to a regular append otherwise.
   105→//
   106→// It is used to assemble the "type<type-id>" and "subtype<stype-id>" selector
   107→// strings when the type tag arrives before the type-id tag in the line, so the
   108→// two byte slices need to be concatenated in tag-declaration order regardless
   109→// of wire order.
   110→func reorder(buf, prefix []byte) []byte {
   111→	n := len(prefix)
   112→	m := len(buf)
   113→	if cap(buf) < m+n {
   114→		return append(prefix[:n:n], buf...)
   115→	} else {
   116→		buf = buf[:n+m]
   117→		for i := m - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
   118→			buf[i+n] = buf[i]
   119→		}
   120→		for i := range n {
   121→			buf[i] = prefix[i]
   122→		}
   123→		return buf
   124→	}
   125→}
   126→
   127→// walDropped counts WAL messages dropped due to a full channel.
   128→// Logged periodically; not critical since binary snapshots capture the data.
   129→var walDropped atomic.Int64
   130→
   131→// decodeState holds the per-call scratch buffers used by DecodeLine.
   132→// Instances are recycled via decodeStatePool to avoid repeated allocations
   133→// during high-throughput ingestion.
   134→type decodeState struct {
   135→	// metricBuf holds a copy of the current measurement name (line-protocol
   136→	// measurement field). Copied because dec.Measurement() returns a slice
   137→	// that is invalidated by the next decoder call.
   138→	metricBuf []byte
   139→
   140→	// selector is the sub-device path passed to WriteToLevel and WALMessage
   141→	// (e.g. ["socket0"] or ["socket0", "memctrl1"]). Reused across lines.
   142→	selector []string
   143→
   144→	// typeBuf accumulates the concatenated "type"+"type-id" tag value for the
   145→	// current line. Reset at the start of each line's tag-decode loop.
   146→	typeBuf []byte
   147→
   148→	// subTypeBuf accumulates the concatenated "subtype"+"stype-id" tag value.
   149→	// Reset at the start of each line's tag-decode loop.
   150→	subTypeBuf []byte
   151→
   152→	// prevTypeBytes / prevTypeStr cache the last seen typeBuf content and its
   153→	// string conversion. Because consecutive lines in a batch typically address
   154→	// the same sub-device, the cache hit rate is very high and avoids
   155→	// repeated []byte→string allocations.
   156→	prevTypeBytes []byte
   157→	prevTypeStr   string
   158→
   159→	// prevSubTypeBytes / prevSubTypeStr are the same cache for the subtype.
   160→	prevSubTypeBytes []byte
   161→	prevSubTypeStr   string
   162→}
   163→
   164→// decodeStatePool recycles decodeState values across DecodeLine calls to
   165→// reduce GC pressure during sustained metric ingestion.
   166→var decodeStatePool = sync.Pool{
   167→	New: func() any {
   168→		return &decodeState{
   169→			metricBuf:  make([]byte, 0, 16),
   170→			selector:   make([]string, 0, 4),
   171→			typeBuf:    make([]byte, 0, 16),
   172→			subTypeBuf: make([]byte, 0, 16),
   173→		}
   174→	},
   175→}
   176→
   177→// DecodeLine reads all lines from dec (InfluxDB line-protocol) and writes each
   178→// decoded metric sample into ms.
   179→//
   180→// clusterDefault is used as the cluster name for lines that do not carry a
   181→// "cluster" tag. Callers typically supply the ClusterTag value from the NATS
   182→// subscription configuration.
   183→//
   184→// Performance notes:
   185→//   - A decodeState is obtained from decodeStatePool to reuse scratch buffers.
   186→//   - The Level pointer (host-level node in the metric tree) is cached across
   187→//     consecutive lines that share the same cluster+host pair to avoid
   188→//     repeated lock acquisitions on the root and cluster levels.
   189→//   - []byte→string conversions for type/subtype selectors are cached via
   190→//     prevType*/prevSubType* fields because batches typically repeat the same
   191→//     sub-device identifiers.
   192→//   - Timestamp parsing tries Second precision first; if that fails it retries
   193→//     Millisecond, Microsecond, and Nanosecond in turn. A missing timestamp
   194→//     falls back to time.Now().
   195→//
   196→// When the checkpoint format is "wal" each successfully decoded sample is also
   197→// sent to WALMessages so the WAL staging goroutine can persist it durably
   198→// before the next binary snapshot.
   199→func DecodeLine(dec *lineprotocol.Decoder,
   200→	ms *MemoryStore,
   201→	clusterDefault string,
   202→) error {
   203→	// Reduce allocations in loop:
   204→	t := time.Now()
   205→	metric := Metric{}
   206→	st := decodeStatePool.Get().(*decodeState)
   207→	defer decodeStatePool.Put(st)
   208→
   209→	// Optimize for the case where all lines in a "batch" are about the same
   210→	// cluster and host. By using `WriteToLevel` (level = host), we do not need
   211→	// to take the root- and cluster-level lock as often.
   212→	var lvl *Level = nil
   213→	prevCluster, prevHost := "", ""
   214→
   215→	var ok bool
   216→	for dec.Next() {
   217→		rawmeasurement, err := dec.Measurement()
   218→		if err != nil {
   219→			return err
   220→		}
   221→
   222→		// Needs to be copied because another call to dec.* would
   223→		// invalidate the returned slice.
   224→		st.metricBuf = append(st.metricBuf[:0], rawmeasurement...)
   225→
   226→		// The go compiler optimizes map[string(byteslice)] lookups:
   227→		metric.MetricConfig, ok = ms.Metrics[string(rawmeasurement)]
   228→		if !ok {
   229→			continue
   230→		}
   231→
   232→		st.typeBuf, st.subTypeBuf = st.typeBuf[:0], st.subTypeBuf[:0]
   233→		cluster, host := clusterDefault, ""
   234→		for {
   235→			key, val, err := dec.NextTag()
   236→			if err != nil {
   237→				return err
   238→			}
   239→			if key == nil {
   240→				break
   241→			}
   242→
   243→			// The go compiler optimizes string([]byte{...}) == "...":
   244→			switch string(key) {
   245→			case "cluster":
   246→				if string(val) == prevCluster {
   247→					cluster = prevCluster
   248→				} else {
   249→					cluster = string(val)
   250→					lvl = nil
   251→				}
   252→			case "hostname", "host":
   253→				if string(val) == prevHost {
   254→					host = prevHost
   255→				} else {
   256→					host = string(val)
   257→					lvl = nil
   258→				}
   259→			case "type":
   260→				if string(val) == "node" {
   261→					break
   262→				}
   263→
   264→				// We cannot be sure that the "type" tag comes before the "type-id" tag:
   265→				if len(st.typeBuf) == 0 {
   266→					st.typeBuf = append(st.typeBuf, val...)
   267→				} else {
   268→					st.typeBuf = reorder(st.typeBuf, val)
   269→				}
   270→			case "type-id":
   271→				st.typeBuf = append(st.typeBuf, val...)
   272→			case "subtype":
   273→				// We cannot be sure that the "subtype" tag comes before the "stype-id" tag:
   274→				if len(st.subTypeBuf) == 0 {
   275→					st.subTypeBuf = append(st.subTypeBuf, val...)
   276→				} else {
   277→					st.subTypeBuf = reorder(st.subTypeBuf, val)
   278→				}
   279→			case "stype-id":
   280→				st.subTypeBuf = append(st.subTypeBuf, val...)
   281→			default:
   282→			}
   283→		}
   284→
   285→		// If the cluster or host changed, the lvl was set to nil
   286→		if lvl == nil {
   287→			st.selector = st.selector[:2]
   288→			st.selector[0], st.selector[1] = cluster, host
   289→			lvl = ms.GetLevel(st.selector)
   290→			prevCluster, prevHost = cluster, host
   291→		}
   292→
   293→		// subtypes: cache []byte→string conversions; messages in a batch typically
   294→		// share the same type/subtype so the hit rate is very high.
   295→		st.selector = st.selector[:0]
   296→		if len(st.typeBuf) > 0 {
   297→			if !bytes.Equal(st.typeBuf, st.prevTypeBytes) {
   298→				st.prevTypeBytes = append(st.prevTypeBytes[:0], st.typeBuf...)
   299→				st.prevTypeStr = string(st.typeBuf)
   300→			}
   301→			st.selector = append(st.selector, st.prevTypeStr)
   302→			if len(st.subTypeBuf) > 0 {
   303→				if !bytes.Equal(st.subTypeBuf, st.prevSubTypeBytes) {
   304→					st.prevSubTypeBytes = append(st.prevSubTypeBytes[:0], st.subTypeBuf...)
   305→					st.prevSubTypeStr = string(st.subTypeBuf)
   306→				}
   307→				st.selector = append(st.selector, st.prevSubTypeStr)
   308→			}
   309→		}
   310→
   311→		for {
   312→			key, val, err := dec.NextField()
   313→			if err != nil {
   314→				return err
   315→			}
   316→
   317→			if key == nil {
   318→				break
   319→			}
   320→
   321→			if string(key) != "value" {
   322→				return fmt.Errorf("host %s: unknown field: '%s' (value: %#v)", host, string(key), val)
   323→			}
   324→
   325→			if val.Kind() == lineprotocol.Float {
   326→				metric.Value = schema.Float(val.FloatV())
   327→			} else if val.Kind() == lineprotocol.Int {
   328→				metric.Value = schema.Float(val.IntV())
   329→			} else if val.Kind() == lineprotocol.Uint {
   330→				metric.Value = schema.Float(val.UintV())
   331→			} else {
   332→				return fmt.Errorf("host %s: unsupported value type in message: %s", host, val.Kind().String())
   333→			}
   334→		}
   335→
   336→		if t, err = dec.Time(lineprotocol.Second, t); err != nil {
   337→			t = time.Now()
   338→			if t, err = dec.Time(lineprotocol.Millisecond, t); err != nil {
   339→				t = time.Now()
   340→				if t, err = dec.Time(lineprotocol.Microsecond, t); err != nil {
   341→					t = time.Now()
   342→					if t, err = dec.Time(lineprotocol.Nanosecond, t); err != nil {
   343→						return fmt.Errorf("host %s: timestamp : %#v with error : %#v", host, t, err.Error())
   344→					}
   345→				}
   346→			}
   347→		}
   348→
   349→		if err != nil {
   350→			return fmt.Errorf("host %s: timestamp : %#v with error : %#v", host, t, err.Error())
   351→		}
   352→
   353→		time := t.Unix()
   354→
   355→		if Keys.Checkpoints.FileFormat == "wal" {
   356→			msg := &WALMessage{
   357→				MetricName: string(st.metricBuf),
   358→				Cluster:    cluster,
   359→				Node:       host,
   360→				Selector:   append([]string{}, st.selector...),
   361→				Value:      metric.Value,
   362→				Timestamp:  time,
   363→			}
   364→			if !SendWALMessage(msg) {
   365→				// WAL shard channel full — metric is written to memory store but not WAL.
   366→				// Next binary snapshot will capture it.
   367→				if dropped := walDropped.Add(1); dropped%10000 == 1 {
   368→					cclog.Warnf("[METRICSTORE]> WAL shard channel full, dropped %d messages (data safe in memory, next snapshot will capture)", dropped)
   369→				}
   370→			}
   371→		}
   372→
   373→		if err := ms.WriteToLevel(lvl, st.selector, time, []Metric{metric}); err != nil {
   374→			cclog.Warnf("write error for host %s metric %s at ts %d: %s", host, string(st.metricBuf), time, err.Error())
   375→			continue
   376→		}
   377→	}
   378→	return nil
   379→}

     6→// Package metricstore provides an efficient in-memory time-series metric storage system
     7→// with support for hierarchical data organization, checkpointing, and archiving.
     8→//
     9→// The package organizes metrics in a tree structure (cluster → host → component) and
    10→// provides concurrent read/write access to metric data with configurable aggregation strategies.
    11→// Background goroutines handle periodic checkpointing (JSON or Avro format), archiving old data,
    12→// and enforcing retention policies.
    13→//
    14→// Key features:
    15→//   - In-memory metric storage with configurable retention
    16→//   - Hierarchical data organization (selectors)
    17→//   - Concurrent checkpoint/archive workers
    18→//   - Support for sum and average aggregation
    19→//   - NATS integration for metric ingestion
    20→package metricstore
    21→
    22→import (
    23→	"bytes"
    24→	"context"
    25→	"encoding/json"
    26→	"errors"
    27→	"fmt"
    28→	"runtime"
    29→	"runtime/debug"
    30→	"slices"
    31→	"sync"
    32→	"time"
    33→
    34→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/config"
    35→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    36→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/resampler"
    37→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    38→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/util"
    39→)
    40→
    41→// GlobalState holds the global state for the metric store with thread-safe access.
    42→type GlobalState struct {
    43→	mu                sync.RWMutex
    44→	lastRetentionTime int64
    45→	selectorsExcluded bool
    46→}
    47→
    48→var (
    49→	singleton  sync.Once
    50→	msInstance *MemoryStore
    51→	// shutdownFunc stores the context cancellation function created in Init
    52→	// and is called during Shutdown to cancel all background goroutines
    53→	shutdownFunc   context.CancelFunc
    54→	shutdownFuncMu sync.Mutex // Protects shutdownFunc from concurrent access
    55→	// Create a global instance
    56→	state = &GlobalState{}
    57→)
    58→
    59→// NodeProvider provides information about nodes currently in use by running jobs.
    60→//
    61→// This interface allows metricstore to query job information without directly
    62→// depending on the repository package, breaking the import cycle.
    63→//
    64→// Implementations should return nodes that are actively processing jobs started
    65→// before the given timestamp. These nodes will be excluded from retention-based
    66→// garbage collection to prevent data loss for jobs that are still running or
    67→// recently completed.
    68→type NodeProvider interface {
    69→	// GetUsedNodes returns a map of cluster names to sorted lists of unique hostnames
    70→	// that are currently in use by jobs that started before the given timestamp.
    71→	//
    72→	// Parameters:
    73→	//   - ts: Unix timestamp threshold - returns nodes with jobs started before this time
    74→	//
    75→	// Returns:
    76→	//   - Map of cluster names to lists of node hostnames that should be excluded from garbage collection
    77→	//   - Error if the query fails
    78→	GetUsedNodes(ts int64) (map[string][]string, error)
    79→}
    80→
    81→// Metric represents a single metric data point to be written to the store.
    82→type Metric struct {
    83→	Name  string
    84→	Value schema.Float
    85→	// MetricConfig contains frequency and aggregation settings for this metric.
    86→	// If Frequency is 0, configuration will be looked up from MemoryStore.Metrics during Write().
    87→	MetricConfig MetricConfig
    88→}
    89→
    90→// MemoryStore is the main in-memory time-series metric storage implementation.
    91→//
    92→// It organizes metrics in a hierarchical tree structure where each level represents
    93→// a component of the system hierarchy (e.g., cluster → host → CPU). Each level can
    94→// store multiple metrics as time-series buffers.
    95→//
    96→// The store is initialized as a singleton via InitMetrics() and accessed via GetMemoryStore().
    97→// All public methods are safe for concurrent use.
    98→type MemoryStore struct {
    99→	Metrics      map[string]MetricConfig
   100→	root         Level
   101→	nodeProvider NodeProvider
   102→}
   103→
   104→// Init initializes the metric store from configuration and starts background workers.
   105→//
   106→// This function must be called exactly once before any other metricstore operations.
   107→// It performs the following initialization steps:
   108→//  1. Validates and decodes the metric store configuration
   109→//  2. Configures worker pool size (defaults to NumCPU/2+1, max 10)
   110→//  3. Loads metric configurations from all registered clusters
   111→//  4. Restores checkpoints within the retention window
   112→//  5. Starts background workers for retention, checkpointing, archiving, and monitoring
   113→//  6. Optionally subscribes to NATS for real-time metric ingestion
   114→//
   115→// Parameters:
   116→//   - rawConfig: JSON configuration for the metric store (see MetricStoreConfig)
   117→//   - wg: WaitGroup that will be incremented for each background goroutine started
   118→//
   119→// The function will call cclog.Fatal on critical errors during initialization.
   120→// Use Shutdown() to cleanly stop all background workers started by Init().
   121→//
   122→// Note: Signal handling must be implemented by the caller. Call Shutdown() when
   123→// receiving termination signals to ensure checkpoint data is persisted.
   124→func Init(rawConfig json.RawMessage, metrics map[string]MetricConfig, wg *sync.WaitGroup) {
   125→	startupTime := time.Now()
   126→
   127→	if rawConfig != nil {
   128→		config.Validate(configSchema, rawConfig)
   129→		dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(rawConfig))
   130→		dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
   131→		if err := dec.Decode(&Keys); err != nil {
   132→			cclog.Abortf("[METRICSTORE]> Metric Store Config Init: Could not decode config file '%s'.\nError: %s\n", rawConfig, err.Error())
   133→		}
   134→	}
   135→
   136→	// Set NumWorkers from config or use default
   137→	if Keys.NumWorkers <= 0 {
   138→		Keys.NumWorkers = min(runtime.NumCPU()/2+1, DefaultMaxWorkers)
   139→	}
   140→	cclog.Debugf("[METRICSTORE]> Using %d workers for checkpoint/archive operations\n", Keys.NumWorkers)
   141→
   142→	// Pass the config.MetricStoreKeys
   143→	InitMetrics(metrics)
   144→
   145→	ms := GetMemoryStore()
   146→
   147→	d, err := time.ParseDuration(Keys.RetentionInMemory)
   148→	if err != nil {
   149→		cclog.Fatal(err)
   150→	}
   151→
   152→	restoreFrom := startupTime.Add(-d)
   153→	cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> Loading checkpoints newer than %s\n", restoreFrom.Format(time.RFC3339))
   154→	files, err := ms.FromCheckpointFiles(Keys.Checkpoints.RootDir, restoreFrom.Unix())
   155→	loadedData := ms.SizeInBytes() / 1024 / 1024 // In MB
   156→	if err != nil {
   157→		cclog.Fatalf("[METRICSTORE]> Loading checkpoints failed: %s\n", err.Error())
   158→	} else {
   159→		cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> Checkpoints loaded (%d files, %d MB, that took %fs)\n", files, loadedData, time.Since(startupTime).Seconds())
   160→	}
   161→
   162→	// Try to use less memory by forcing a GC run here and then
   163→	// lowering the target percentage. The default of 100 means
   164→	// that only once the ratio of new allocations execeds the
   165→	// previously active heap, a GC is triggered.
   166→	// Forcing a GC here will set the "previously active heap"
   167→	// to a minumum.
   168→	// runtime.GC()
   169→
   170→	ctx, shutdown := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
   171→
   172→	Retention(wg, ctx)
   173→	Checkpointing(wg, ctx)
   174→	CleanUp(wg, ctx)
   175→	WALStaging(wg, ctx)
   176→	MemoryUsageTracker(wg, ctx)
   177→
   178→	// Note: Signal handling has been removed from this function.
   179→	// The caller is responsible for handling shutdown signals and calling
   180→	// the shutdown() function when appropriate.
   181→	// Store the shutdown function for later use by Shutdown()
   182→	shutdownFuncMu.Lock()
   183→	shutdownFunc = shutdown
   184→	shutdownFuncMu.Unlock()
   185→
   186→	if Keys.Subscriptions != nil {
   187→		wg.Go(func() {
   188→			if err := ReceiveNats(ms, Keys.NumWorkers, ctx); err != nil {
   189→				cclog.Fatal(err)
   190→			}
   191→		})
   192→	}
   193→}
   194→
   195→// InitMetrics initializes the singleton MemoryStore instance with the given metric configurations.
   196→//
   197→// This function must be called before GetMemoryStore() and can only be called once due to
   198→// the singleton pattern. It assigns each metric an internal offset for efficient buffer indexing.
   199→//
   200→// Parameters:
   201→//   - metrics: Map of metric names to their configurations (frequency and aggregation strategy)
   202→//
   203→// Panics if any metric has Frequency == 0, which indicates an invalid configuration.
   204→//
   205→// After this call, the global msInstance is ready for use via GetMemoryStore().
   206→func InitMetrics(metrics map[string]MetricConfig) {
   207→	singleton.Do(func() {
   208→		offset := 0
   209→		for key, cfg := range metrics {
   210→			if cfg.Frequency == 0 {
   211→				panic("[METRICSTORE]> invalid frequency")
   212→			}
   213→
   214→			metrics[key] = MetricConfig{
   215→				Frequency:   cfg.Frequency,
   216→				Aggregation: cfg.Aggregation,
   217→				offset:      offset,
   218→			}
   219→			offset += 1
   220→		}
   221→
   222→		msInstance = &MemoryStore{
   223→			root: Level{
   224→				metrics:  make([]*buffer, len(metrics)),
   225→				children: make(map[string]*Level),
   226→			},
   227→			Metrics: metrics,
   228→		}
   229→	})
   230→}
   231→
   232→// GetMemoryStore returns the singleton MemoryStore instance.
   233→//
   234→// Returns the initialized MemoryStore singleton. Calls cclog.Fatal if InitMetrics() was not called first.
   235→//
   236→// This function is safe for concurrent use after initialization.
   237→func GetMemoryStore() *MemoryStore {
   238→	if msInstance == nil {
   239→		cclog.Warnf("[METRICSTORE]> MemoryStore not initialized!")
   240→	}
   241→
   242→	return msInstance
   243→}
   244→
   245→func (ms *MemoryStore) GetMetricFrequency(metricName string) (int64, error) {
   246→	if metric, ok := ms.Metrics[metricName]; ok {
   247→		return metric.Frequency, nil
   248→	}
   249→	return 0, fmt.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> metric %s not found", metricName)
   250→}
   251→
   252→// SetNodeProvider sets the NodeProvider implementation for the MemoryStore.
   253→// This must be called during initialization to provide job state information
   254→// for selective buffer retention during Free operations.
   255→// If not set, the Free function will fall back to freeing all buffers.
   256→func (ms *MemoryStore) SetNodeProvider(provider NodeProvider) {
   257→	ms.nodeProvider = provider
   258→}
   259→
   260→// Shutdown performs a graceful shutdown of the metric store.
   261→//
   262→// This function cancels all background goroutines started by Init() and writes
   263→// a final checkpoint to disk before returning. It should be called when the
   264→// application receives a termination signal.
   265→//
   266→// The function will:
   267→//  1. Cancel the context to stop all background workers
   268→//  2. Close the WAL messages channel if using WAL format
   269→//  3. Write a final checkpoint to preserve in-memory data
   270→//  4. Log any errors encountered during shutdown
   271→//
   272→// Note: This function blocks until the final checkpoint is written.
   273→func Shutdown() {
   274→	totalStart := time.Now()
   275→
   276→	shutdownFuncMu.Lock()
   277→	defer shutdownFuncMu.Unlock()
   278→	if shutdownFunc != nil {
   279→		shutdownFunc()
   280→	}
   281→	cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> Background workers cancelled (%v)", time.Since(totalStart))
   282→
   283→	if Keys.Checkpoints.FileFormat == "wal" {
   284→		for _, ch := range walShardChs {
   285→			close(ch)
   286→		}
   287→		drainStart := time.Now()
   288→		WaitForWALStagingDrain()
   289→		cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> WAL staging goroutines exited (%v)", time.Since(drainStart))
   290→	}
   291→
   292→	cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> Writing checkpoint to '%s'...", Keys.Checkpoints.RootDir)
   293→	checkpointStart := time.Now()
   294→	var files int
   295→	var err error
   296→
   297→	ms := GetMemoryStore()
   298→
   299→	lastCheckpointMu.Lock()
   300→	from := lastCheckpoint
   301→	lastCheckpointMu.Unlock()
   302→
   303→	if Keys.Checkpoints.FileFormat == "wal" {
   304→		// WAL files are deleted per-host inside ToCheckpointWAL workers.
   305→		files, _, err = ms.ToCheckpointWAL(Keys.Checkpoints.RootDir, from.Unix(), time.Now().Unix())
   306→	} else {
   307→		files, err = ms.ToCheckpoint(Keys.Checkpoints.RootDir, from.Unix(), time.Now().Unix())
   308→	}
   309→
   310→	if err != nil {
   311→		cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> Writing checkpoint failed: %s", err.Error())
   312→	}
   313→	cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> Done! (%d files written in %v, total shutdown: %v)", files, time.Since(checkpointStart), time.Since(totalStart))
   314→}
   315→
   316→// Retention starts a background goroutine that periodically frees old metric data.
   317→//
   318→// This worker runs at half the retention interval and calls Free() to remove buffers
   319→// older than the configured retention time. It respects the NodeProvider to preserve
   320→// data for nodes with active jobs.
   321→//
   322→// Parameters:
   323→//   - wg: WaitGroup to signal completion when context is cancelled
   324→//   - ctx: Context for cancellation signal
   325→//
   326→// The goroutine exits when ctx is cancelled.
   327→func Retention(wg *sync.WaitGroup, ctx context.Context) {
   328→	ms := GetMemoryStore()
   329→
   330→	wg.Go(func() {
   331→		d, err := time.ParseDuration(Keys.RetentionInMemory)
   332→		if err != nil {
   333→			cclog.Fatal(err)
   334→		}
   335→		if d <= 0 {
   336→			return
   337→		}
   338→
   339→		tickInterval := d / 2
   340→		if tickInterval <= 0 {
   341→			return
   342→		}
   343→		ticker := time.NewTicker(tickInterval)
   344→		defer ticker.Stop()
   345→
   346→		for {
   347→			select {
   348→			case <-ctx.Done():
   349→				return
   350→			case <-ticker.C:
   351→				state.mu.Lock()
   352→
   353→				t := time.Now().Add(-d)
   354→
   355→				state.lastRetentionTime = t.Unix()
   356→
   357→				cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> start freeing buffers (older than %s)...\n", t.Format(time.RFC3339))
   358→
   359→				freed, err := Free(ms, t)
   360→				if err != nil {
   361→					cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> freeing up buffers failed: %s\n", err.Error())
   362→				} else {
   363→					cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> done: %d buffers freed\n", freed)
   364→				}
   365→
   366→				state.mu.Unlock()
   367→
   368→				// Clean up the buffer pool
   369→				bufferPool.Clean(state.lastRetentionTime)
   370→			}
   371→		}
   372→	})
   373→}
   374→
   375→// MemoryUsageTracker starts a background goroutine that monitors memory usage.
   376→//
   377→// This worker checks actual process memory usage (via runtime.MemStats) periodically
   378→// and force-frees buffers if memory exceeds the configured cap. It uses FreeOSMemory()
   379→// to return memory to the OS after freeing buffers, avoiding aggressive GC that causes
   380→// performance issues.
   381→//
   382→// The tracker logs both actual memory usage (heap allocated) and metric data size for
   383→// visibility into memory overhead from Go runtime structures and allocations.
   384→//
   385→// Parameters:
   386→//   - wg: WaitGroup to signal completion when context is cancelled
   387→//   - ctx: Context for cancellation signal
   388→//
   389→// The goroutine exits when ctx is cancelled.
   390→func MemoryUsageTracker(wg *sync.WaitGroup, ctx context.Context) {
   391→	ms := GetMemoryStore()
   392→
   393→	wg.Go(func() {
   394→		normalInterval := DefaultMemoryUsageTrackerInterval
   395→		fastInterval := 30 * time.Second
   396→
   397→		if normalInterval <= 0 {
   398→			return
   399→		}
   400→
   401→		ticker := time.NewTicker(normalInterval)
   402→		defer ticker.Stop()
   403→		currentInterval := normalInterval
   404→
   405→		for {
   406→			select {
   407→			case <-ctx.Done():
   408→				return
   409→			case <-ticker.C:
   410→				var mem runtime.MemStats
   411→				runtime.ReadMemStats(&mem)
   412→				actualMemoryGB := float64(mem.Alloc) / 1e9
   413→				metricDataGB := ms.SizeInGB()
   414→				cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> memory usage: %.2f GB actual (%.2f GB metric data)", actualMemoryGB, metricDataGB)
   415→
   416→				freedExcluded := 0
   417→				freedEmergency := 0
   418→				var err error
   419→
   420→				state.mu.RLock()
   421→				lastRetention := state.lastRetentionTime
   422→				selectorsExcluded := state.selectorsExcluded
   423→				state.mu.RUnlock()
   424→
   425→				if lastRetention != 0 && selectorsExcluded {
   426→					freedExcluded, err = ms.Free(nil, lastRetention)
   427→					if err != nil {
   428→						cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> error while force-freeing the excluded buffers: %s", err)
   429→					}
   430→
   431→					if freedExcluded > 0 {
   432→						debug.FreeOSMemory()
   433→						cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> done: %d excluded buffers force-freed", freedExcluded)
   434→					}
   435→				}
   436→
   437→				runtime.ReadMemStats(&mem)
   438→				actualMemoryGB = float64(mem.Alloc) / 1e9
   439→
   440→				if actualMemoryGB > float64(Keys.MemoryCap) {
   441→					cclog.Warnf("[METRICSTORE]> memory usage %.2f GB exceeds cap %d GB, starting emergency buffer freeing", actualMemoryGB, Keys.MemoryCap)
   442→
   443→					// Use progressive time-based Free with increasing threshold
   444→					// instead of ForceFree loop — fewer tree traversals, more effective
   445→					d, parseErr := time.ParseDuration(Keys.RetentionInMemory)
   446→					if parseErr != nil {
   447→						cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> cannot parse retention duration: %s", parseErr)
   448→					} else {
   449→						thresholds := []float64{0.75, 0.5, 0.25}
   450→						for _, fraction := range thresholds {
   451→							threshold := time.Now().Add(-time.Duration(float64(d) * fraction))
   452→							freed, freeErr := ms.Free(nil, threshold.Unix())
   453→							if freeErr != nil {
   454→								cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> error while freeing buffers at %.0f%% retention: %s", fraction*100, freeErr)
   455→							}
   456→							freedEmergency += freed
   457→
   458→							bufferPool.Clear()
   459→							runtime.GC()
   460→							runtime.ReadMemStats(&mem)
   461→							actualMemoryGB = float64(mem.Alloc) / 1e9
   462→
   463→							if actualMemoryGB < float64(Keys.MemoryCap) {
   464→								break
   465→							}
   466→						}
   467→					}
   468→
   469→					bufferPool.Clear()
   470→					debug.FreeOSMemory()
   471→
   472→					runtime.ReadMemStats(&mem)
   473→					actualMemoryGB = float64(mem.Alloc) / 1e9
   474→
   475→					if actualMemoryGB >= float64(Keys.MemoryCap) {
   476→						cclog.Errorf("[METRICSTORE]> after emergency frees (%d buffers), memory usage %.2f GB still at/above cap %d GB", freedEmergency, actualMemoryGB, Keys.MemoryCap)
   477→					} else {
   478→						cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> emergency freeing complete: %d buffers freed, memory now %.2f GB", freedEmergency, actualMemoryGB)
   479→					}
   480→				}
   481→
   482→				// Adaptive ticker: check more frequently when memory is high
   483→				memoryRatio := actualMemoryGB / float64(Keys.MemoryCap)
   484→				if memoryRatio > 0.8 && currentInterval != fastInterval {
   485→					ticker.Reset(fastInterval)
   486→					currentInterval = fastInterval
   487→					cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> memory at %.0f%% of cap, switching to fast check interval (30s)", memoryRatio*100)
   488→				} else if memoryRatio <= 0.8 && currentInterval != normalInterval {
   489→					ticker.Reset(normalInterval)
   490→					currentInterval = normalInterval
   491→					cclog.Infof("[METRICSTORE]> memory at %.0f%% of cap, switching to normal check interval", memoryRatio*100)
   492→				}
   493→			}
   494→		}
   495→	})
   496→}
   497→
   498→// Free removes metric data older than the given time while preserving data for active nodes.
   499→//
   500→// This function implements intelligent retention by consulting the NodeProvider (if configured)
   501→// to determine which nodes are currently in use by running jobs. Data for these nodes is
   502→// preserved even if older than the retention time.
   503→//
   504→// Parameters:
   505→//   - ms: The MemoryStore instance
   506→//   - t: Time threshold - buffers with data older than this will be freed
   507→//
   508→// Returns:
   509→//   - Number of buffers freed
   510→//   - Error if NodeProvider query fails
   511→//
   512→// Behavior:
   513→//   - If no NodeProvider is set: frees all buffers older than t
   514→//   - If NodeProvider returns empty map: frees all buffers older than t
   515→//   - Otherwise: preserves buffers for nodes returned by GetUsedNodes(), frees others
   516→func Free(ms *MemoryStore, t time.Time) (int, error) {
   517→	// If no NodeProvider is configured, free all buffers older than t
   518→	if ms.nodeProvider == nil {
   519→		return ms.Free(nil, t.Unix())
   520→	}
   521→
   522→	excludeSelectors, err := ms.nodeProvider.GetUsedNodes(t.Unix())
   523→	if err != nil {
   524→		return 0, err
   525→	}
   526→
   527→	switch lenMap := len(excludeSelectors); lenMap {
   528→
   529→	// If the length of the map returned by GetUsedNodes() is 0,
   530→	// then use default Free method with nil selector
   531→	case 0:
   532→		state.selectorsExcluded = false
   533→		return ms.Free(nil, t.Unix())
   534→
   535→	// Else formulate selectors, exclude those from the map
   536→	// and free the rest of the selectors
   537→	default:
   538→		state.selectorsExcluded = true
   539→		selectors := GetSelectors(ms, excludeSelectors)
   540→		return FreeSelected(ms, selectors, t)
   541→	}
   542→}
   543→
   544→// FreeSelected frees buffers for specific selectors while preserving others.
   545→//
   546→// This function is used when we want to retain some specific nodes beyond the retention time.
   547→// It iterates through the provided selectors and frees their associated buffers.
   548→//
   549→// Parameters:
   550→//   - ms: The MemoryStore instance
   551→//   - selectors: List of selector paths to free (e.g., [["cluster1", "node1"], ["cluster2", "node2"]])
   552→//   - t: Time threshold for freeing buffers
   553→//
   554→// Returns the total number of buffers freed and any error encountered.
   555→func FreeSelected(ms *MemoryStore, selectors [][]string, t time.Time) (int, error) {
   556→	freed := 0
   557→
   558→	for _, selector := range selectors {
   559→
   560→		freedBuffers, err := ms.Free(selector, t.Unix())
   561→		if err != nil {
   562→			cclog.Errorf("error while freeing selected buffers: %#v", err)
   563→		}
   564→		freed += freedBuffers
   565→
   566→	}
   567→
   568→	return freed, nil
   569→}
   570→
   571→// GetSelectors returns all selectors at depth 2 (cluster/node level) that are NOT in the exclusion map.
   572→//
   573→// This function generates a list of selectors whose buffers should be freed by excluding
   574→// selectors that correspond to nodes currently in use by running jobs.
   575→//
   576→// Parameters:
   577→//   - ms: The MemoryStore instance
   578→//   - excludeSelectors: Map of cluster names to node hostnames that should NOT be freed
   579→//
   580→// Returns a list of selectors ([]string paths) that can be safely freed.
   581→//
   582→// Example:
   583→//
   584→//	If the tree has paths ["emmy", "node001"] and ["emmy", "node002"],
   585→//	and excludeSelectors contains {"emmy": ["node001"]},
   586→//	then only [["emmy", "node002"]] is returned.
   587→func GetSelectors(ms *MemoryStore, excludeSelectors map[string][]string) [][]string {
   588→	allSelectors := ms.GetPaths(2)
   589→
   590→	filteredSelectors := make([][]string, 0, len(allSelectors))
   591→
   592→	for _, path := range allSelectors {
   593→		if len(path) < 2 {
   594→			continue
   595→		}
   596→
   597→		key := path[0]   // The "Key" (Level 1)
   598→		value := path[1] // The "Value" (Level 2)
   599→
   600→		exclude := false
   601→
   602→		// Check if the key exists in our exclusion map
   603→		if excludedValues, exists := excludeSelectors[key]; exists {
   604→			// The key exists, now check if the specific value is in the exclusion list
   605→			if slices.Contains(excludedValues, value) {
   606→				exclude = true
   607→			}
   608→		}
   609→
   610→		if !exclude {
   611→			filteredSelectors = append(filteredSelectors, path)
   612→		}
   613→	}
   614→
   615→	return filteredSelectors
   616→}
   617→
   618→// GetPaths returns a list of lists (paths) to the specified depth.
   619→func (ms *MemoryStore) GetPaths(targetDepth int) [][]string {
   620→	var results [][]string
   621→
   622→	// Start recursion. Initial path is empty.
   623→	// We treat Root as depth 0.
   624→	ms.root.collectPaths(0, targetDepth, []string{}, &results)
   625→
   626→	return results
   627→}
   628→
   629→// Write all values in `metrics` to the level specified by `selector` for time `ts`.
   630→// Look at `findLevelOrCreate` for how selectors work.
   631→func (m *MemoryStore) Write(selector []string, ts int64, metrics []Metric) error {
   632→	var ok bool
   633→	for i, metric := range metrics {
   634→		if metric.MetricConfig.Frequency == 0 {
   635→			metric.MetricConfig, ok = m.Metrics[metric.Name]
   636→			if !ok {
   637→				cclog.Debugf("[METRICSTORE]> Unknown metric '%s' in Write() - skipping", metric.Name)
   638→				metric.MetricConfig.Frequency = 0
   639→			}
   640→			metrics[i] = metric
   641→		}
   642→	}
   643→
   644→	return m.WriteToLevel(&m.root, selector, ts, metrics)
   645→}
   646→
   647→func (m *MemoryStore) GetLevel(selector []string) *Level {
   648→	return m.root.findLevelOrCreate(selector, len(m.Metrics))
   649→}
   650→
   651→// WriteToLevel assumes that `minfo` in `metrics` is filled in
   652→func (m *MemoryStore) WriteToLevel(l *Level, selector []string, ts int64, metrics []Metric) error {
   653→	l = l.findLevelOrCreate(selector, len(m.Metrics))
   654→	l.lock.Lock()
   655→	defer l.lock.Unlock()
   656→
   657→	for _, metric := range metrics {
   658→		if metric.MetricConfig.Frequency == 0 {
   659→			continue
   660→		}
   661→
   662→		b := l.metrics[metric.MetricConfig.offset]
   663→		if b == nil {
   664→			// First write to this metric and level
   665→			b = newBuffer(ts, metric.MetricConfig.Frequency)
   666→			l.metrics[metric.MetricConfig.offset] = b
   667→		}
   668→
   669→		nb, err := b.write(ts, metric.Value)
   670→		if err != nil {
   671→			return err
   672→		}
   673→
   674→		// Last write created a new buffer...
   675→		if b != nb {
   676→			l.metrics[metric.MetricConfig.offset] = nb
   677→		}
   678→	}
   679→	return nil
   680→}
   681→
   682→// Read returns all values for metric `metric` from `from` to `to` for the selected level(s).
   683→// If the level does not hold the metric itself, the data will be aggregated recursively from the children.
   684→// The second and third return value are the actual from/to for the data. Those can be different from
   685→// the range asked for if no data was available.
   686→func (m *MemoryStore) Read(selector util.Selector, metric string, from, to, resolution int64) ([]schema.Float, int64, int64, int64, error) {
   687→	if from > to {
   688→		return nil, 0, 0, 0, errors.New("[METRICSTORE]> invalid time range")
   689→	}
   690→
   691→	minfo, ok := m.Metrics[metric]
   692→	if !ok {
   693→		return nil, 0, 0, 0, errors.New("[METRICSTORE]> unknown metric: " + metric)
   694→	}
   695→
   696→	n, data := 0, make([]schema.Float, (to-from)/minfo.Frequency+1)
   697→
   698→	err := m.root.findBuffers(selector, minfo.offset, func(b *buffer) error {
   699→		cdata, cfrom, cto, err := b.read(from, to, data)
   700→		if err != nil {
   701→			return err
   702→		}
   703→
   704→		if n == 0 {
   705→			from, to = cfrom, cto
   706→		} else if from != cfrom || to != cto || len(data) != len(cdata) {
   707→			missingfront, missingback := int((from-cfrom)/minfo.Frequency), int((to-cto)/minfo.Frequency)
   708→			if missingfront != 0 {
   709→				return ErrDataDoesNotAlign
   710→			}
   711→
   712→			newlen := len(cdata) - missingback
   713→			if newlen < 1 {
   714→				return ErrDataDoesNotAlign
   715→			}
   716→			cdata = cdata[0:newlen]
   717→			if len(cdata) != len(data) {
   718→				return ErrDataDoesNotAlign
   719→			}
   720→
   721→			from, to = cfrom, cto
   722→		}
   723→
   724→		data = cdata
   725→		n += 1
   726→		return nil
   727→	})
   728→
   729→	if err != nil {
   730→		return nil, 0, 0, 0, err
   731→	} else if n == 0 {
   732→		return nil, 0, 0, 0, ErrNoHostOrMetric
   733→	} else if n > 1 {
   734→		if minfo.Aggregation == AvgAggregation {
   735→			normalize := 1. / schema.Float(n)
   736→			for i := 0; i < len(data); i++ {
   737→				data[i] *= normalize
   738→			}
   739→		} else if minfo.Aggregation != SumAggregation {
   740→			return nil, 0, 0, 0, errors.New("[METRICSTORE]> invalid aggregation")
   741→		}
   742→	}
   743→
   744→	data, resolution, err = resampler.LargestTriangleThreeBucket(data, minfo.Frequency, resolution)
   745→	if err != nil {
   746→		return nil, 0, 0, 0, err
   747→	}
   748→
   749→	return data, from, to, resolution, nil
   750→}
   751→
   752→// Free releases all buffers for the selected level and all its children that
   753→// contain only values older than `t`.
   754→func (m *MemoryStore) Free(selector []string, t int64) (int, error) {
   755→	return m.GetLevel(selector).free(t)
   756→}
   757→
   758→// ForceFree unconditionally removes the oldest buffer from each metric chain.
   759→func (m *MemoryStore) ForceFree() (int, error) {
   760→	return m.GetLevel(nil).forceFree()
   761→}
   762→
   763→func (m *MemoryStore) FreeAll() error {
   764→	m.root.lock.Lock()
   765→	defer m.root.lock.Unlock()
   766→
   767→	for k := range m.root.children {
   768→		delete(m.root.children, k)
   769→	}
   770→
   771→	return nil
   772→}
   773→
   774→func (m *MemoryStore) SizeInBytes() int64 {
   775→	return m.root.sizeInBytes()
   776→}
   777→
   778→func (m *MemoryStore) SizeInGB() float64 {
   779→	return float64(m.root.sizeInBytes()) / 1e9
   780→}
   781→
   782→// ListChildren , given a selector, returns a list of all children of the level
   783→// selected.
   784→func (m *MemoryStore) ListChildren(selector []string) []string {
   785→	lvl := &m.root
   786→	for lvl != nil && len(selector) != 0 {
   787→		lvl.lock.RLock()
   788→		next := lvl.children[selector[0]]
   789→		lvl.lock.RUnlock()
   790→		lvl = next
   791→		selector = selector[1:]
   792→	}
   793→
   794→	if lvl == nil {
   795→		return nil
   796→	}
   797→
   798→	lvl.lock.RLock()
   799→	defer lvl.lock.RUnlock()
   800→
   801→	children := make([]string, 0, len(lvl.children))
   802→	for child := range lvl.children {
   803→		children = append(children, child)
   804→	}
   805→
   806→	return children
   807→}
   808→
